cap_space_post_fa: ~$0-3000000 (extremely tight; Brown/Gardeck deals will reduce remaining room significantly)

key_departures.impact: Etienne was the Jaguars' workhorse back, posting 1,107 rushing yards and 7 TDs plus 36 receptions for 292 yards and 6 receiving TDs in 2025. He signed a 4-year, $52M deal with the Saints. Jacksonville opted not to franchise tag him (tag value ~$14.5M) and allowed him to walk. Bhayshul Tuten and LeQuint Allen remain but neither is a proven starter.

Lloyd was the Jaguars' defensive signal-caller and their best defensive player in 2025 — 2nd-team All-Pro, Pro Bowl, 5 interceptions including a 99-yard pick-six off Mahomes, 81 tackles, 6 pass deflections, 6 TFLs. He signed a 3-year, $45M deal ($25M guaranteed) with Carolina. The Jaguars declined to extend him off his rookie deal and could not meet his market. His departure leaves a massive hole at the green-dot linebacker position.
Smoot was a rotational EDGE who provided depth behind Travon Walker and Josh Hines-Allen. His departure creates a depth hole but the starters return.
Veteran rotational pass rusher. 12 career sacks, 1 sack in 2025. Age-related decline. Depth loss but not a major hole given Gardeck's return.
One-year depth signing from 2025 who struggled. Brown's re-signing largely offsets this departure. CB room still crowded with Hunter, Brown, Lewis, Jones, Braswell.
Veteran depth receiver who appeared in limited games. Loss is minimal given Jacksonville's deep WR room (Brian Thomas Jr., Jakobi Meyers locked up long-term, Travis Hunter).
Depth receiver on a one-year deal. Minimal roster impact.
Veteran interior DL rotational piece. Loss compounds existing IDL/pass-rush need, though DaVon Hamilton and Arik Armstead remain (Armstead restructure/release pending).
Special teams core and depth safety. Loss is modest given return of Eric Murray and Antonio Johnson at safety, plus Caleb Ransaw returning from IR.

signings.analysis: Brown was the Jaguars' CB2 starter opposite Travis Hunter and came off his best season — 12 passes defensed (team-high), 2 INTs, 47 tackles, and a 69.6 passer rating in coverage (6th-best among CBs with 200+ coverage snaps per ESPN NGS). The 3-year deal at $11M AAV keeps him below his market value (reportedly could have commanded $12M+/yr) through his prime. This was JAX's most critical internal re-signing. CB is now fully addressed with Hunter, Brown, Jourdan Lewis (nickel), Jarrian Jones, and Christian Braswell all under contract. CB drops from a potential Tier 1 need to a Tier 4/5 — no meaningful draft investment needed.
signings.sources_note: Confirmed by ESPN/Adam Schefter and AP on March 9, 2026

signings.analysis: Gardeck returned from ACL surgery to post 2.5 sacks, 48 tackles, 6 TFLs, and 9 QB hits in 17 games (6 starts) in 2025. Re-signing him at $6M AAV (incentive-heavy, limited guarantees per reports) retains a quality rotational pass rusher. However, this does NOT solve the EDGE depth problem left by Smoot and Ogbah departing. Gardeck is 31, coming off major knee surgery, and is a depth/rotational piece — not a starter. JAX still needs an additional EDGE body, especially with Devin Lloyd gone (leaving fewer LB blitz packages). EDGE need drops one tier but remains an active need.
signings.sources_note: Confirmed by official Jaguars announcement March 9; terms per Big Cat Country/Read Optional ($12M/2yr)

signings.analysis: Morris emerged as a key pass-blocking TE in 2025 (5 starts, 6 receptions, 1 TD). His re-signing on a 1-year, up-to-$3M deal is a depth/continuity move, not a long-term solution. The Jaguars' TE room is thin at the top — Brenton Strange is the nominal starter but injury-prone. One-year deal signals Morris is a stop-gap. TE need remains; a mid-round draft pick at TE is still very possible.
signings.sources_note: Confirmed by Jordan Schultz/NFL Network and official Jaguars tracker; terms per Schultz ($3M up to)

signings.analysis: Dallas was re-signed for special teams and emergency depth (3 games, 2 carries, 6 KR). This does NOT address the RB need created by Etienne's departure. Bhayshul Tuten and LeQuint Allen are the projected starters, but neither has proven they can carry a 260-carry starting role. JAX needs a veteran RB addition either in free agency (now or wave 2) or the draft. RB remains a significant need.
signings.sources_note: Confirmed by official Jaguars tracker (re-signed Feb 16); exact terms estimated at vet minimum/near-minimum level based on role

signings.analysis: Dickerson was a late-season revelation who earned 4 starts and contributed to the #1 rush defense. His 1-year, $1.45M deal (qualifying for Veteran Salary Benefit, $1.225M cap hit per Spotrac) is purely a depth retention move. With Austin Johnson also gone, JAX still desperately needs a starting-caliber IDL pass-rusher. Interior pressure was the team's #1 weakness (ranked 3rd in pressures but 27th in sacks). Dickerson minimally offsets IDL attrition but the need remains critical.
signings.sources_note: Confirmed by Spotrac on February 20, 2026 with exact terms

signings.analysis: ERFA tender keeps the 2023 6th-round pick (14 tackles, 3 PDs in 17 games) in fold as developmental CB depth. Low cost, no meaningful need impact.
signings.sources_note: Confirmed by official Jaguars tracker (re-signed Feb 19)

signings.analysis: RFA tender retains a CB depth piece. With Brown re-signed and Travis Hunter returning, Taylor provides rotational depth only. Not a need-mover.
signings.sources_note: Confirmed by official Jaguars tracker as RFA tender

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Devin Lloyd's departure to the Panthers (3yr/$45M/$25M guaranteed) is the single biggest loss of Jacksonville's offseason. Lloyd was the defensive signal-caller, 2nd-team All-Pro, and the engine of the Jaguars' 2025 defense (5 INTs, 81 tackles, 6 PDs). No replacement was signed. Gardeck's re-signing is a pass-rusher, not a coverage LB. The Jaguars have no proven every-down ILB on the roster. This is now the clearest draft priority — rounds 1-3 range.

Travis Etienne's departure (Saints, 4yr/$52M) leaves a gaping hole at RB. Etienne posted 1,107 rushing yards and 13 total TDs in 2025. Bhayshul Tuten and LeQuint Allen are unproven as lead backs — Tuten had 83 carries as a rookie and Allen is a third-down specialist. DeeJay Dallas is a special teams-only player. JAX needs a veteran starter or high-upside draft pick at RB. Need jumps from a pre-FA non-issue to Tier 2.

IDL interior pass rush was already the team's #1 structural weakness going into FA (27th in sacks, 3rd in pressures). Austin Johnson's departure worsens the depth picture. Dickerson's 1-year minimum deal is depth filler. Arik Armstead ($19.4M cap hit) may still be restructured or released. No meaningful IDL addition was made in the first wave. Need remains Tier 2 — team should add a starting-caliber IDL in free agency (wave 2) or the first 3 rounds of the draft.

Gardeck's 2yr/$12M re-signing partially backfills Smoot/Ogbah departures, but Gardeck is 31 and incentive-heavy at a depth/rotational level. Walker and Hines-Allen remain the starting duo. EDGE stays Tier 3 — the starters are fine, depth is still thin, a developmental day 3 pick or wave-2 FA makes sense but it's not urgent.

Brown's 3yr/$33M re-signing (confirmed starter, 11M AAV, below-market rate) paired with Travis Hunter's sophomore year at CB, Jourdan Lewis in the slot, Jarrian Jones, and Braswell makes CB one of Jacksonville's deepest position groups. No first-round draft capital needed here. CB drops from Tier 2 concern to Tier 4 — only developmental/special-teams depth needed.

Morris's 1-year re-signing provides continuity but no upgrade. Brenton Strange remains the starter but has missed time with injuries. The TE room is thin enough that a mid-round draft pick (rounds 3-5) remains viable. Need is unchanged at Tier 3.

key_additions.headline: Homegrown starter returns on below-market deal, locking down CB2 spot opposite Travis Hunter through his prime
Veteran pass rusher (2.5 sacks, 9 QB hits in 2025) returns to provide EDGE depth behind Walker and Hines-Allen
Pass-blocking TE and special teams ace returns on one-year deal after emerging as a key piece mid-season
Special teams ace and emergency RB depth retained at veteran minimum
Late-season IDL revelation re-signed at minimum; earned 4 starts on the league's top run defense in 2025

key_losses.headline: Franchise workhorse departs to hometown Saints after 1,107-yard, 13-TD season — biggest offensive loss of the offseason
2nd-team All-Pro signal-caller walks after breakthrough 2025 season (5 INTs, 99-yd pick-six), leaving a massive void at ILB
Rotational EDGE departs, thinning an already-undersized pass-rush rotation behind the starting duo
Veteran EDGE depth piece exits, compounding pass-rush depth concerns
Rotational IDL departs, worsening interior pass-rush depth on a unit already ranked 27th in sacks in 2025

notes: Free agency for JAX is in its very early stages (legal tampering period opened March 9; league year officially starts March 12). All signings listed are confirmed via official team announcements or credibly reported by multiple Tier 1 sources (Schefter/ESPN, Rapoport/NFL Network, AP). Contract guarantees for Brown ($18M est.) and Dallas/Dickerson minimums are estimated where exact guarantee figures were not publicly disclosed. Gardeck's contract was confirmed at 2yr/$12M by Read Optional/Big Cat Country but described as incentive-heavy with limited guarantees — treat guarantees as TBD. The Jaguars entered FA week with just ~$6.9M in cap space after multiple restructures, severely limiting their ability to sign external free agents. Expect continued cap-management moves (possible Armstead restructure/release) before any significant external additions. Travis Hunter's role expansion at CB in 2026 (noted by GM Gladstone) is a factor in CB need assessment.
